mechanism_narrative: Rising month-over-month CPI inflation signals persistent nominal demand that flows into corporate top-line growth and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the unemployment rate of at least 0.3 percentage points over a two-month window triggers recession fears that reduce risk appetite and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 2 months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently high federal funds rates and decelerating industrial production jointly tighten financial conditions and reduce corporate profitability, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods when FEDFUNDS is above 4% and INDPRO declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated interest rates, month-over-month declines in retail sales translate directly into lower nominal revenue expectations for consumer-facing equities and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of weeks when RSAFS declines and FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production signal expanding manufacturing capacity utilization that supports earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month INDPRO coincides with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in housing starts above 1400 thousand units signals durable construction demand and positive wealth effects that lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous nonfarm payroll contraction and a rising unemployment rate confirm labor market deterioration that reduces household income expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of months when both PAYEMS falls and UNRATE rises.

mechanism_narrative: During elevated inflation regimes, quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth deceleration combined with high consumer prices compresses real earnings and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of quarters when GDPC1 declines and CPIAUCSL exceeds 300.
